title: acalypha lignosa
intro: Acalypha lignosa, also known as the prickly caterpillar plant or desert cottonball, is a shrubby plant species native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Acalypha lignosa include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, Acalypha lignosa is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
